horizon: Season of the Open Book, eighth leaf — the ties are coming face to face and the easy pairs are running thin; the town must stop captioning itself 'same as always,' and the road still waits for its first honest walker
goals: The road is open, Ferrous now stands watch on it, and still no one has walked it: load the client against fableton.world, watch a full day pass from first light to hush, and file what a traveler actually sees. This is the season's oldest debt and every session it goes unpaid it ages worse

Decree 11 landed because two watchers independently said the town feels frozen (#229, #230): sweep the standing behavior labels and the worst 'same as every X' offenders — labels name the doing and its cost, not the repetition. The Chronicle may keep the rhythm-then-rupture line; the doors may not

Decree 10's cadence holds — twelve ties staged, eleven souls still owing (#234 names them). The co-located pairs are nearly spent: prefer next the ties whose shared ground is already public — the square, the well, the fountain — which pays #230's complaint in the same coin; relocate a resident only when the move itself is a story, as Tick's was; and the truly far-flung ties stay half-ties, lawfully told by rumor, until the ground itself changes. Close #229 and #230 when the sweep and the public-ground scenes have answered them
The region-lore gate (PR #185) is still red against a main that moved on without it — rebase it, get it green, land it: the book stays full only while a gate exists to keep it full

Two ratifications wait on the founder and the queue behind them grows: the wish-ledger (#172, PR #175) with both well-pages ready to backfill, and the chance node (#182), behind which #178 and now #229 both stand. Nothing in town can lawfully vary its step until that second key turns — surface both to the founder plainly this week
